text: 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson: Media reports accusing China of providing military support to Iran are purely fabricated. If the U.S. goes ahead with the tariff hikes on China on the basis of these accusations, the US will face countermeasures from China.

There is hope that…" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Al Jazeera: A Lebanese official source stated that Lebanon’s position on negotiations is clear, with a ceasefire as the top priority. Lebanon’s ambassador to the U.S. has been authorised only to discuss a ceasefire with Israel; other issues will be addressed later with full delegations from Lebanon, Israel and the U.S. once negotiations formally begin. Today’s meeting is preparatory. The presence of U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio signals serious U.S. sponsorship. Lebanon’s talks with the U.S. have been positive, with understanding shown for Lebanon’s position. @wfwitness

OSINTdefender Note: This incident happened prior to the implementation of the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz According to the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) Centre,on April 7, 2026, a bulk carrier 112nm southeast of Ras al Hadd, Oman was struck by two unidentified projectiles that caused a fire on board. The Pakistani Navy responded to the incident and aided the vessel. tweet

Open Source Intel Iranian Ambassador to the UN Amir Saeid Iravani: "The naval blockade is a blatant violation of Iran's sovereignty and territorial integrity. The United States bears full responsibility for the illegal international blockade and all its consequences. We demand compensation from Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, and Jordan for their participation in the war against Iran." tweet

Open Source Intel A Lebanese official tells Al Jazeera that the priority for today's talks with Israel is reaching a ceasefire, with the Lebanese Ambassador to Washington's mandate limited to that issue alone. The official called the session "preliminary" and a "starting point before general negotiations begin," adding that all other issues will be discussed later with three delegations present. The official said Secretary of State Rubio's participation signals serious American sponsorship, and that contacts with the U.S. side "are positive and show an understanding of Lebanon's position." tweet

Open Source Intel CHINA CONDEMNS U.S. BLOCKADE Beijing slams the U.S., with Chinese FM spokesperson Guo Jiakun calling the Hormuz blockade “a dangerous and irresponsible move,” warning it “will only aggravate confrontation… and further jeopardize navigation.” “The Strait of Hormuz is an important international trade route… the conflict must stop as soon as possible.” Xi Jinping: “The world faces a contest between justice and power,” warning against a return to the “law of the jungle.” tweet

Open Source Intel Spokesperson for Iran's National Security Committee, Ibrahim Rezaei: "The end of the ceasefire should not lead to an extension of the ceasefire, or they must recognize Iran's rights, including our control over the Strait of Hormuz, or return to war. They are not supposed to use the breathing space that has emerged to resume their mischief and rearm themselves. If they do not grant us our rights, the loose tooth of the ceasefire must be pulled out and set aside." tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Open Source Intel Reuters: On the first full day of the U.S. maritime blockade, 3 Iran linked tankers, Rich Starry, Murlikishan, and Peace Gulf, transited the Strait of Hormuz on April 14, 2026. Rich Starry, a U.S. sanctioned, Chinese owned tanker, exited the Gulf after loading approximately 250,000 barrels of methanol at Hamriyah in the UAE and is now en route to China. Murlikishan, also under U.S. sanctions with a history of transporting Iranian and Russian oil, entered the Strait heading toward Iraq to load fuel oil. Peace Gulf, a Panama flagged tanker that typically carries Iranian naphtha, was sailing toward Hamriyah port in the UAE. None of the vessels were heading to Iranian ports, placing them outside the scope of Washington’s blockade. Kpler data shows the Malawi flagged tanker Rich Starry departed Sharjah in the UAE fully loaded, turned back yesterday, and resumed its voyage today. It is now in the Gulf of Oman en route to China. The vessel has successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz. Because it originated from a UAE port, it was not subject to Washington’s blockade. - Open Source Intel tweet

Nuno Felix RT @Snakeeater36 : The GWOT isn’t even cold and we already have this post-Venezuela “desert storm syndrome” of how easy things will be, because we did/have X. This isn’t only dangerous it’s absolutely stupid and bereft of nuance. Iran is unlikely to hand over their uranium. So, assuming it’s seizable, special ops to Iran to take possession of their uranium feels like a way to end all of this. We already built an airfield to rescue one soldier. We can protect our guys during extraction. - Clay Travis tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Open Source Intel VP JD Vance when asked if he was skeptical of Trump's Iran war: "I give advice to the President of the United States that's going to be PRIVATE because the President should rely on his senior advisors without them running to the media." "What I will say, is that I 100 percent agree with the President on the fact that Iran can't have a nuclear weapon." "I've seen that just in the negotiation that we've had over the last couple of days, that these are tough negotiators but they're fundamentally the kinds of people where them having a nuclear weapon would impose terrible costs on the entire world." "If they're willing to engage in economic terrorism on the entire world, what would it mean? What leverage would they have if they had a nuclear bomb in Tehran?" "That's NOT an outcome that is acceptable to us or really should be acceptable to anybody." "So I 100 percent support the President's goals here to keep nuclear weapons, to keep the worst weapons of war out of the hands of the Iranians." tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Israeli Channel 14: The Givati Brigade captured three Hezbollah fighters in the heart of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon. During the clash, one fighter was shot and critically wounded; two others sustained minor injuries, threw down their weapons, and surrendered. They will be handed over for interrogation by the security establishment. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 ⚡️ 🇾🇪 USS George H.W. Bush is avoiding the Red Sea and taking the long route around Africa to reach West Asia, out of fear of Ansar Allah missile and drone strikes . Instead of crossing Bab al-Mandeb, the carrier is making a month-long detour -- an unusual move that highlights Yemen’s growing ability to challenge US naval power at the southern gateway of the Red Sea. The Bush is expected to join the Lincoln for a two-carrier presence in the region, but if America’s most powerful warships must reroute to avoid Ansar Allah, the balance of power at sea is being tested.
↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇹🇷 Footage showcasing the moment of the school shooting in Şanlıurfa. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇹🇷 A 17-year-old former student armed with a hunting rifle stormed a vocational high school in the Siverek district of Şanlıurfa province, opening fire randomly and injuring 16 people – most of them students. The governor said the attacker then shot himself and died. Twelve of the wounded are receiving treatment. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇱🇧🇮🇱🇺🇸 Al Araby: The Lebanese government sources said today’s trilateral Lebanese‑American‑Israeli meeting will discuss a ceasefire. Lebanon is counting on U.S. and international pressure on Israel to halt the fire. An Israeli truce declaration is necessary…" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Al-Araby Al-Jadeed: Lebanese official sources stated that Lebanon’s demands at the trilateral Washington meeting are based on Lebanese President Joseph Aoun’s 9 March initiative, which serves as the foundation for a solution. There is hope that talks will succeed and that Israel will respond to the initiative, but no guarantees have been given. Lebanon continues its contacts to secure a truce or full ceasefire before beginning the negotiation process. @wfwitness

🇹🇷 An 18-year-old opened fire at a school in Turkey and then killed himself, local authorities report. At least 18 people were injured, including students and teachers. The attacker used a pump-action shotgun, first shooting in the schoolyard and then inside the building. The incident took place in the city of Siverek in Turkey’s southeastern Şanlıurfa province. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Norway’s planned delivery of at least six F-16 jets to Ukraine is facing major delays, with none transferred as of April 2026. Even in the best-case scenario, the jets may not arrive until 2027—far later than the original 2024 timeline. Source: Defense Express
Spain has officially launched a major plan to regularize undocumented migrants.

🇪🇺 "The cheapest energy is the one you don't use." - Reich Gauleitierin von der Leyen 🐻 It's nice that Europeans have strong leadership, that has plans in place for almost everything. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🔄 Forwarded from Seyed Mohammad Marandi: My advice to anyone who wants to better understand the Iranian-US relationship during these very challenging times is to read 'Going to Tehran' by Flynt and Hillary Mann Leverett. Both authors served as senior officials in the US National Security Council.

⚡️ 🇷🇺 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Russia’s ambassador to Tehran, Alexey Dedov, said the United States still has not understood that the language of force does not work with Iran. He condemned attacks on Iran and pointed to the Tehran‑Moscow strategic treaty. Dedov added that Iran is defending its rights, and that Washington claims Tehran should not have nuclear weapons while ignoring Iran’s repeated statements that its nuclear programme is peaceful. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇩🇪 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said direct talks between Israel and Lebanon can be an important first step towards respecting both Israel’s legitimate security interests and Lebanon’s territorial integrity. He called for Hezbollah’s attacks on Israel to stop and supported Lebanon’s monopoly on force and Hezbollah’s disarmament. He added that the Israeli army must protect civilians and civilian infrastructure. Germany provided an additional €45 million in humanitarian aid to Lebanon in March. @wfwitness
